docs.title: Welcome to Rectangle2D
docs.text: Rectangle2D is an simple Lua-driven 2D game engine made in C/C++. In its early versions it was developed using Python. Rectangle2D uses raylib as rendering engine tho Rectangle2D adds alot.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
1. Simple lua intergation
  2. Project is activly maintained
  3. Games built on Rectangle2D are fast!
  4. Built-in easy to use IDE.

docs.title: How to start?
docs.text: I recommend starting off from reading Getting Started, Then checking out Tutorials After that you can checkout Samples for more stuff, API Refrence does have some examples but they are small and not as usefull as ones you can find in Tutorials or Samples.

docs.title: Credits
docs.text:
• FreeToPlayUser - Creator of Rectangle 2D
  • Roberto Ierusalimschy - Creator of lua
  • raysan5 - Creator of raylib

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
First of all you will need to build or download Rectangle2D it self, Downloads can be found here. But to build the project you will make you will need to build or download RectangleCompile from here

After this, I recommend to use this sample project. As it makes it very quick to create something, But if you don't want to use sample project but instead want to make it you'r self I recommend checking out API Refrence and Samples or maybe even Tutorials But this is you'r choice.

docs.title: Writing a simple game
docs.text:
So after you download or make you'r own project, you should find main.lua somewhere. It should look somewhat like this: 

InitWindow(800, 600, "Hello World")

while not WindowShouldClose() do
    BeginDrawing()
    ClearBackground(0, 0, 0)

    EndDrawing()
end

But if it dosen't, Thats ok just make sure you call all of these functions (Except WindowShouldClose() as its optional and can be replaced with infinite loop).

Now let's add an Rectangle in middle of the screen, To do this we make Object like this:

local rectangle = CreateObject("Rectangle", 400, 300, 100, 100, 0)

First 2 arguments are Postion X and Y, Second 2 are Size X and Y and last one is rotation. Just to make it clear, We should call this function after InitWindow but not in the loop.

Ok great, now we have rectangle right? You might see that it dosen't show yet. To show it (Or draw) we should call rectangle:Draw() In our loop.

After our edits the code should look like this:

InitWindow(800, 600, "Hello World")

local rectangle = CreateObject("Rectangle", 400, 300, 100, 100, 0)

while not WindowShouldClose() do
    BeginDrawing()
    ClearBackground(0, 0, 0)

    rectangle:Draw()

    EndDrawing()
end

docs.title: Compiling a game
docs.text:
If it does, thats great! Next step we should do is compiling the project. To compile an project you will need that compiler from earlier. You should have this file Compiler.exe. Now by now you you have folder in which you have main.lua. Now place the Compiler.exe, lua53.dll, WindowsClient.exe into ../ folder of you'r game folder.

Now run this command to compile you'r game:

Compiler <full path to you'r game folder> <full path to you'r game build folder>\Game.exe

You should see Game.exe alongside lua53.dll in you'r game build folder, Thats you'r compiled game!

docs.title: Samples
docs.text: Here you can find many Rectangle2D game samples using which you can learn how to make a game! But before we jump into game samples let me show you basic applications.

docs.title: Sample list
docs.text:
• Movement
  • Colors & Textures
  • Input
  • Audio

docs.title: Tutorials
docs.text: Here you can find many tutorials that can help you do stuff.

docs.title: Structuring a game
docs.text:
Structure in Rectangle2D game is very easy but also important, Its required to properly compile you'r cool game! Without it, you can't compile a game.

So here is an example of proper structure:

.
├── CoolGame/
│   └── main.lua
├── Compiler.exe
├── WindowsClient.exe
└── lua53.dll

You don't need to place CoolGame folder exactly where you'r compiler is but this was done to demonstrate how it should look. And make sure to place assets and other .lua files in you'r game folder (In this case, CoolGame). And that's it!

docs.title: Compiling a game
docs.text: To actually play a game made in Rectangle2D you need to compile it, its very easy, you just need to follow these steps carefully.

docs.title: Preparing game folder
docs.text:
First of all let me show you, how it could look.

.
├── CoolGame/
│   └── main.lua
├── CompiledGame/
│   └── nothing here yet
├── Compiler.exe
├── WindowsClient.exe
└── lua53.dll

This is an example game structure.

docs.title: Compiling it
docs.text:
So now that we have our game folder prepared, lets'ts build our game. To do this we need to run this command:

Compiler.exe <full path to CoolGame folder> <full path to CompiledGame folder>\Build.exe

Just to be clear you can name Build.exe anything. After running this command you should see 2 files in CompiledGame folder, It should look like this by now: 

.
├── CoolGame/
│   └── main.lua
├── CompiledGame/
│   ├── Build.exe
│   └── lua53.dll
├── Compiler.exe
├── WindowsClient.exe
└── lua53.dll

If you don't see these 2 files, This means something went wrong in compilation, To make it clear compilation dosen't check for invalid functions etc as by it self compilation in Rectangle2D is just these 2 steps: 1. Compile lua bytecode 2. Pack the game And for now that's it!

docs.title: Audio play thingy
docs.text:
Plays audio when game is open.

InitWindow(800, 600, "Audio")
InitAudio()

local rectangle = CreateObject("Rectangle", 400, 300, 100, 100, 0)

local music = Audio.New("music.wav")
music:Play()

while not WindowShouldClose() do
    BeginDrawing()
    ClearBackground(0, 0, 0)

    rectangle:Draw()
    EndDrawing()
end

docs.title: Set random volume
docs.text:
Yeah uh this is just demo to show SetVolume.

InitWindow(800, 600, "Audio")
InitAudio()

local rectangle = CreateObject("Rectangle", 400, 300, 100, 100, 0)

local music = Audio.New("music.wav")
music:Play()

while not WindowShouldClose() do
    BeginDrawing()
    ClearBackground(0, 0, 0)

    math.randomseed(os.time()) 
    music:SetVolume(math.random(1, 100))

    rectangle:Draw()
    EndDrawing()
end

docs.title: Stop Audio Randomly
docs.text:
Stop audio randomly, i made this to show :Stop() function.

InitWindow(800, 600, "Audio")
InitAudio()

local rectangle = CreateObject("Rectangle", 400, 300, 100, 100, 0)

local music = Audio.New("music.wav")
music:Play()

while not WindowShouldClose() do
    BeginDrawing()
    ClearBackground(0, 0, 0)

    math.randomseed(os.time())
    if math.random(1, 100) <= 80 then
        music:Stop()
    end

    rectangle:Draw()
    EndDrawing()
end

docs.title: Blue Rectangle
docs.text:
A blue rectangle in the center.

InitWindow(800, 600, "Rectangle")

local Blue = Material.New(0, 0, 255, 255) -- Red, Green, Blue, Alpha

local rectangle = CreateObject("Rectangle", 400, 300, 100, 100, 0)
rectangle:SetMaterial(Blue)

while not WindowShouldClose() do
    BeginDrawing()
    ClearBackground(0, 0, 0)

    rectangle:Draw()

    EndDrawing()
end

docs.title: Textured Rectangle
docs.text:
Simple textured rectangle in the center.

InitWindow(800, 600, "Rectangle")

local mat = Material.New(0, 0, 0, 255) -- Color and alpha dont matter if material has texture
local texture = Texture.New("Apple.png")
mat:SetTexture(texture)

local rectangle = CreateObject("Rectangle", 400, 300, 100, 100, 0)
rectangle:SetMaterial(mat)

while not WindowShouldClose() do
    BeginDrawing()
    ClearBackground(0, 0, 0)

    rectangle:Draw()

    EndDrawing()
end

docs.title: Rainbow Rectangle
docs.text:
A rainbow rectangle!

InitWindow(800, 600, "Rainbow")

local r, g, b = 255, 0, 0
local time = 0

local RainbowMaterial = Material.New(r, g, b, 255)
local rectangle = CreateObject("Rectangle", 400, 300, 100, 100, 0)
rectangle:SetMaterial(RainbowMaterial)

while not WindowShouldClose() do
    BeginDrawing()
    ClearBackground(0, 0, 0)

    time = time + GetDeltaTime() * 2

    r = (math.sin(time) * 0.5 + 0.5) * 255
    g = (math.sin(time + 2.094) * 0.5 + 0.5) * 255
    b = (math.sin(time + 4.188) * 0.5 + 0.5) * 255

    RainbowMaterial:SetColor(math.floor(r), math.floor(g), math.floor(b), 255)

    rectangle:Draw()

    EndDrawing()
end

docs.title: W/A/S/D Movement
docs.text:
Simple w/a/s/d movement. if you are wondering why i multiply deltatime by 200? I have no idea but it works so don't question it!

InitWindow(800, 600, "W/A/S/D Movement")

local rectangle = CreateObject("Rectangle", 400, 300, 100, 100, 0)

local speed = 1

while not WindowShouldClose() do
    BeginDrawing()
    ClearBackground(0, 0, 0)

    local dtime = GetDeltaTime() * 200

    if Input.IsKeyDown(87) then
        rectangle:SetPosition(rectangle:GetPositionX(), rectangle:GetPositionY() - speed * dtime)
    end

    if Input.IsKeyDown(83) then
        rectangle:SetPosition(rectangle:GetPositionX(), rectangle:GetPositionY() + speed * dtime)
    end

    if Input.IsKeyDown(65) then
        rectangle:SetPosition(rectangle:GetPositionX() - speed * dtime, rectangle:GetPositionY())
    end

    if Input.IsKeyDown(68) then
        rectangle:SetPosition(rectangle:GetPositionX() + speed * dtime, rectangle:GetPositionY())
    end

    rectangle:Draw()

    EndDrawing()
end

docs.title: Mouse Follower
docs.text:
A rectangle that follows you'r mouse

InitWindow(800, 600, "Mouse Follower")

local rectangle = CreateObject("Rectangle", 400, 300, 100, 100, 0)

while not WindowShouldClose() do
    BeginDrawing()
    ClearBackground(0, 0, 0)

    rectangle:SetPosition(Input.GetMouseX(), Input.GetMouseY())
    rectangle:Draw()

    EndDrawing()
end

docs.title: Clicker game
docs.text:
Yayyy, a clicker!

InitWindow(800, 600, "Clicker Game")

local score = 0

local rectangle = CreateObject("Rectangle", 400, 300, 100, 100, 0)
local ScoreText = CreateObject("Text", 325, 150, 100, 100, 0)

ScoreText:SetCustomProperty("text", "Score: ".. score)
ScoreText:SetCustomProperty("fontSize", 40)

function AABB(ax, ay, aw, ah, bx, by, bw, bh)
    local rectX = bx - bw/2
    local rectY = by - bh/2

    return ax < rectX + bw and
           ax + aw > rectX and
           ay < rectY + bh and
           ay + ah > rectY
end

while not WindowShouldClose() do
    BeginDrawing()
    ClearBackground(0, 0, 0)

    local X = Input.MouseX()
    local Y = Input.MouseY()

    if Input.IsMouseButtonPressed(0) then
        if AABB(X, Y, 5, 5, 400, 300, 100, 100) then
            score = score + 1
        end
    end

    ScoreText:SetCustomProperty("text", "Score: ".. score)

    rectangle:Draw()
    ScoreText:Draw()
    EndDrawing()
end

docs.title: Rectangle In The Center
docs.text:
A simple rectangle in center.

InitWindow(800, 600, "Rectangle")

local rectangle = CreateObject("Rectangle", 400, 300, 100, 100, 0)

while not WindowShouldClose() do
    BeginDrawing()
    ClearBackground(0, 0, 0)

    rectangle:Draw()

    EndDrawing()
end

docs.title: Rotating Rectangle
docs.text:
A simple rotating Rectangle.

InitWindow(800, 600, "Rectangle")

local Rotation = 0
local rectangle = CreateObject("Rectangle", 400, 300, 100, 100, Rotation)

while not WindowShouldClose() do
    BeginDrawing()
    ClearBackground(0, 0, 0)

    Rotation = Rotation + 0.1
    rectangle:SetRotation(Rotation)

    rectangle:Draw()

    EndDrawing()
end

docs.title: Moving Rectangle
docs.text:
This rectangle moves up and down.

InitWindow(800, 600, "Rectangle")

local Y = 300
local rectangle = CreateObject("Rectangle", 400, Y, 100, 100, 0)

local direction = true -- true is up, false is down

while not WindowShouldClose() do
    BeginDrawing()
    ClearBackground(0, 0, 0)

    if direction == true then
        Y = Y - 0.1
    end
    if direction == false then
        Y = Y + 0.1
    end

    if Y >= 500 or Y <= 100 then
        direction = not direction
    end

    rectangle:SetPosition(400, Y)

    rectangle:Draw()

    EndDrawing()
end

docs.title: Moving & Rotating Rectangle
docs.text:
This one moves up and down too but also rotates!

InitWindow(800, 600, "Rectangle")

local Rotation = 0
local Y = 300

local rectangle = CreateObject("Rectangle", 400, Y, 100, 100, Rotation)

local direction = true -- true is up, false is down

while not WindowShouldClose() do
    BeginDrawing()
    ClearBackground(0, 0, 0)

    if direction == true then
        Y = Y - 0.1
    end
    if direction == false then
        Y = Y + 0.1
    end

    if Y >= 500 or Y <= 100 then
        direction = not direction
    end

    Rotation = Rotation + 0.1

    rectangle:SetPosition(400, Y)
    rectangle:SetRotation(Rotation)

    rectangle:Draw()

    EndDrawing()
end
